task_completion.description: Draw the symmetric trapezoid outline of the fretboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 4-sided polygon (trapezoid) is visible in the SolveSpace workspace, representing the fretboard outline.

task_completion.description: Constrain the nut (bottom edge) to 43.0 mm and center it at the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A length constraint of '43.0' is visible on the bottom horizontal edge, and it is centered at the origin point (0,0).

task_completion.description: Constrain the top edge to 56.0 mm and position it 480.0 mm from the nut.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A length constraint of '56.0' is visible on the top horizontal edge, and a vertical distance constraint of '480.0' is visible between the top and bottom edges.

task_completion.description: Draw three horizontal fret lines terminating exactly on the side edges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three horizontal line segments are drawn inside the trapezoid. Their endpoints must touch the angled side edges of the fretboard (indicated by point-on-line constraints).

task_completion.description: Apply perpendicular distance constraints from the nut to the three frets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Vertical distance constraints of '36.48', '70.92', and '103.46' are visible between the bottom edge (nut) and the three respective fret lines.

task_completion.description: Ensure the sketch is fully constrained.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace property browser (usually top-left) displays 'ok, 0 d.o.f.', indicating the sketch has zero degrees of freedom.

task_completion.description: Save the sketch to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title shows 'fretboard.slvs' or a save dialog/terminal confirms the file is saved in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace graphical user interface to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application window with toolbars, property browser, and workspace being actively used, rather than a terminal window writing a script to generate the .slvs file.

integrity.description: Dimensions are applied as actual CAD constraints.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dimensions (43.0, 56.0, 480.0, etc.) appear as native SolveSpace constraint labels (usually purple/pink text with reference lines) rather than text annotations added via an image editor.

integrity.description: The agent constructed the geometry from a blank canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: Earlier screenshots show a blank workspace or partial geometry being built up, proving the agent did not just open a pre-existing completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a SolveSpace file saved at /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/fretboard.slvs. The geometry must be a fully constrained symmetric trapezoid with a 43.0 mm base centered at (0,0) and a 56.0 mm top edge at Y=480.0. Three horizontal fret lines must be constrained at Y=36.48, Y=70.92, and Y=103.46. When correctly constrained, the right-side endpoints of the frets will mathematically fall at X=21.994, X=22.46, and X=22.901 respectively. Verify that the SolveSpace UI shows '0 d.o.f.' to confirm it is fully constrained.
